title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
focus: Baseline retrieve-then-generate architecture with dense passage retrieval.
summary: RAG establishes the core pattern of combining parametric generation with non-parametric retrieval and is the simplest paper in the set to explain end to end.
limitations: It does not include retrieval self-correction or explicit critique stages.

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
focus: Adaptive retrieval and response critique through reflection tokens.
summary: Self-RAG expands the standard RAG pattern by letting the model decide when retrieval is needed and critique its own answer quality.
limitations: The method is more complex to train and reason about than a narrow POC usually requires.

title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
focus: Retrieval-quality scoring and corrective evidence refinement.
summary: CRAG is the strongest fit when the main product risk is weak or noisy retrieval rather than generation alone.
limitations: Its corrective pipeline adds orchestration overhead that may be unnecessary for a fixed, curated corpus.
